title: Connection Reuse, Pools, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and Concurrency · Browser and Node Client Systems · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A product accessibility and SDK team. Media contributors need an abortable upload coordinator that streams bounded chunks, resumes from verified offsets, and never reports completion before server state is reconciled. Planned artifact: Deliver Abortable Resumable Upload Coordinator with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Prove request bodies and credential state can be reconstructed before replaying an attempt. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

project case — TypeScript stakeholder SDK schedules 24 operations across HTTP/1.1 keep-alive, an HTTP/2 connection limited to 3 streams, and optional HTTP/3. Bound queues and memory, reuse clients, finish bodies, and distinguish transport from semantics.
